result:
Subject: About yesterday's payments outage

Hi there,

Yesterday, between roughly [start time] and [end time] UTC, our payments service was degraded for about four hours. During that window, some of you saw transactions fail outright — charges that didn't go through, checkouts that wouldn't complete, and retries that compounded the frustration. We're sorry. When you trust us to move money, "mostly working" isn't good enough, and we fell short of that standard.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule that, under a traffic spike, began evicting entries faster than downstream services could repopulate them. That cascaded into timeouts across the payment path. We've reverted the rule, added the correct invalidation bounds, and confirmed the service has been stable since. No payment data was lost or exposed — the failures were transactional, not a data incident.

To make sure this specific class of failure doesn't reach you again, we're rolling out two changes: canary deployments for all cache and routing configuration, so changes hit a small slice of traffic before going broad, and scheduled chaos testing in staging that simulates traffic spikes against new config before release. Both are in flight this week, and we'll share a fuller post-mortem once they're live.

If your account was affected, we'll be applying a goodwill credit automatically over the next few days — no action needed on your end. If you had a failed transaction that caused knock-on issues for your business or customers, please reply to this email and we'll work through it with you directly. Thank you for your patience, and again, we're sorry.

— The [Company] Payments Team
